mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units starts signals a surge in demand for residential construction materials, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production signals a supply chain disruption that compresses profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising initial unemployment claims are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that raises discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP signals a surge in demand for goods and services, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production and a 1-month decline in housing units starts signals a supply chain disruption and a decline in demand for residential construction materials, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production and housing starts are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in initial unemployment claims and a 2-month decline in average hourly earnings signals a weakening labor market and a decline in consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising initial unemployment claims and declining average hourly earnings are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the federal funds rate and a 1-month decline in the consumer price index signals a tightening monetary cycle and a decline in inflation expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate and declining consumer price index are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
